title: Libinhania balfourii
taxonomy: Libinhania balfourii is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asclepiadaceae. This family, now often treated as a subfamily (Periplocoideae), is part of the Apocynaceae family in some classifications. It is native to the Socotra Islands in Yemen. The plant is further classified as a species within the genus Libinhania. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Libinhania balfourii, also known as Egyptian chamomile, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Middle East and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its calming and soothing properties, offering relief from stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as aiding in digestion and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Libinhania balfourii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Libinhania balfourii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in essential oil form to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
